moduleDescription:
A simple XML tree generator. See also:
  • xmlparser module for high-level XML parsing
  • parsexml module for low-level XML parsing
  • htmlgen module for html code generator

entries.name: XmlNode
entries.code: XmlNode = ref XmlNodeObj
entries.description:
An XML tree consisting of XML nodes.

Use newXmlTree proc for creating a new tree.

entries.name: XmlNodeKind
entries.code:
XmlNodeKind = enum
  xnText,                   ## a text element
  xnVerbatimText, xnElement, ## an element with 0 or more children
  xnCData,                  ## a CDATA node
  xnEntity,                 ## an entity (like ``&thing;``)
  xnComment                  ## an XML comment
entries.description: Different kinds of XML nodes.

entries.name: XmlAttributes
entries.code: XmlAttributes = StringTableRef
entries.description:
An alias for a string to string mapping.

Use toXmlAttributes proc to create XmlAttributes.

entries.name: xmlHeader
entries.code: xmlHeader = "<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\" ?>\n"
entries.description: Header to use for complete XML output.

entries.name: newElement
entries.code: proc newElement(tag: sink string): XmlNode {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Creates a new XmlNode of kind xnElement with the given tag.

See also:

  • newXmlTree proc
  • <> macro

entries.name: newText
entries.code: proc newText(text: sink string): XmlNode {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Creates a new XmlNode of kind xnText with the text text.

entries.name: newVerbatimText
entries.code:
proc newVerbatimText(text: sink string): XmlNode {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Creates a new XmlNode of kind xnVerbatimText with the text text. Since: Version 1.3.

entries.name: newComment
entries.code:
proc newComment(comment: sink string): XmlNode {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Creates a new XmlNode of kind xnComment with the text comment.

entries.name: newCData
entries.code: proc newCData(cdata: sink string): XmlNode {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Creates a new XmlNode of kind xnCData with the text cdata.

entries.name: newEntity
entries.code: proc newEntity(entity: string): XmlNode {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Creates a new XmlNode of kind xnEntity with the text entity.

entries.name: newXmlTree
entries.description:
Creates a new XML tree with tag, children and attributes.

See also:

  • newElement proc
  • <> macro

entries.name: text
entries.code: proc text(n: XmlNode): lent string {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Gets the associated text with the node n.

n can be a CDATA, Text, comment, or entity node.

See also:

  • text= proc for text setter
  • tag proc for tag getter
  • tag= proc for tag setter
  • innerText proc

entries.name: text=
entries.code:
proc text=(n: XmlNode; text: sink string) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Sets the associated text with the node n.

n can be a CDATA, Text, comment, or entity node.

See also:

  • text proc for text getter
  • tag proc for tag getter
  • tag= proc for tag setter

entries.name: tag
entries.code: proc tag(n: XmlNode): lent string {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Gets the tag name of n.

n has to be an xnElement node.

See also:

  • text proc for text getter
  • text= proc for text setter
  • tag= proc for tag setter
  • innerText proc

entries.name: tag=
entries.code:
proc tag=(n: XmlNode; tag: sink string) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Sets the tag name of n.

n has to be an xnElement node.

See also:

  • text proc for text getter
  • text= proc for text setter
  • tag proc for tag getter

entries.name: rawText
entries.code: proc rawText(n: XmlNode): string {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns the underlying 'text' string by reference.

This is only used for speed hacks.

entries.name: rawTag
entries.code: proc rawTag(n: XmlNode): string {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns the underlying 'tag' string by reference.

This is only used for speed hacks.

entries.name: innerText
entries.code: proc innerText(n: XmlNode): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Gets the inner text of n:
  • If n is xnText or xnEntity, returns its content.
  • If n is xnElement, runs recursively on each child node and concatenates the results.
  • Otherwise returns an empty string.

See also:

  • text proc

entries.name: add
entries.code: proc add(father, son: XmlNode) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Adds the child son to father. father must be of xnElement type

See also:

  • add proc
  • insert proc
  • insert proc
  • delete proc
  • delete proc
  • replace proc
  • replace proc

entries.name: add
entries.code:
proc add(father: XmlNode; sons: openArray[XmlNode]) {.inline, raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Adds the children sons to father. father must be of xnElement type

See also:

  • add proc
  • insert proc
  • insert proc
  • delete proc
  • delete proc
  • replace proc
  • replace proc

entries.name: insert
entries.code:
proc insert(father, son: XmlNode; index: int) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Inserts the child son to a given position in father.

father must be of xnElement kind.

See also:

  • insert proc
  • add proc
  • add proc
  • delete proc
  • delete proc
  • replace proc
  • replace proc

entries.name: insert
entries.code:
proc insert(father: XmlNode; sons: openArray[XmlNode]; index: int) {.inline,
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Inserts the children openArray`sons` to a given position in father.

father must be of xnElement kind.

See also:

  • insert proc
  • add proc
  • add proc
  • delete proc
  • delete proc
  • replace proc
  • replace proc

entries.name: delete
entries.code: proc delete(n: XmlNode; i: Natural) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Deletes the i'th child of n.

See also:

  • delete proc
  • add proc
  • add proc
  • insert proc
  • insert proc
  • replace proc
  • replace proc

entries.name: delete
entries.code: proc delete(n: XmlNode; slice: Slice[int]) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Deletes the items n[slice] of n.

See also:

  • delete proc
  • add proc
  • add proc
  • insert proc
  • insert proc
  • replace proc
  • replace proc

entries.name: replace
entries.code:
proc replace(n: XmlNode; i: Natural; replacement: openArray[XmlNode]) {.
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Replaces the i'th child of n with replacement openArray.

n must be of xnElement kind.

See also:

  • replace proc
  • add proc
  • add proc
  • delete proc
  • delete proc
  • insert proc
  • insert proc

entries.name: replace
entries.code:
proc replace(n: XmlNode; slice: Slice[int]; replacement: openArray[XmlNode]) {.
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Deletes the items n[slice] of n.

n must be of xnElement kind.

See also:

  • replace proc
  • add proc
  • add proc
  • delete proc
  • delete proc
  • insert proc
  • insert proc

entries.name: len
entries.code: proc len(n: XmlNode): int {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the number of n's children.

entries.name: kind
entries.code: proc kind(n: XmlNode): XmlNodeKind {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns n's kind.

entries.code:
proc `[]`(n: XmlNode; i: int): XmlNode {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the i'th child of n.

entries.code:
proc `[]`(n: var XmlNode; i: int): var XmlNode {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the i'th child of n so that it can be modified.

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear(n: var XmlNode) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Recursively clears all children of an XmlNode.

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items(n: XmlNode): XmlNode {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates over all direct children of n.

entries.name: mitems
entries.code:
iterator mitems(n: var XmlNode): var XmlNode {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates over all direct children of n so that they can be modified.

entries.name: toXmlAttributes
entries.code:
proc toXmlAttributes(keyValuePairs: varargs[tuple[key, val: string]]): XmlAttributes {.
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts {key: value} pairs into XmlAttributes.

entries.name: attrs
entries.code:
proc attrs(n: XmlNode): XmlAttributes {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
                                        forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Gets the attributes belonging to n.

Returns nil if attributes have not been initialised for this node.

See also:

  • attrs= proc for XmlAttributes setter
  • attrsLen proc for number of attributes
  • attr proc for finding an attribute

entries.name: attrs=
entries.code:
proc attrs=(n: XmlNode; attr: XmlAttributes) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Sets the attributes belonging to n.

See also:

  • attrs proc for XmlAttributes getter
  • attrsLen proc for number of attributes
  • attr proc for finding an attribute

entries.name: attrsLen
entries.code: proc attrsLen(n: XmlNode): int {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns the number of n's attributes.

See also:

  • attrs proc for XmlAttributes getter
  • attrs= proc for XmlAttributes setter
  • attr proc for finding an attribute

entries.name: attr
entries.code: proc attr(n: XmlNode; name: string): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Finds the first attribute of n with a name of name. Returns "" on failure.

See also:

  • attrs proc for XmlAttributes getter
  • attrs= proc for XmlAttributes setter
  • attrsLen proc for number of attributes

entries.name: clientData
entries.code: proc clientData(n: XmlNode): int {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Gets the client data of n.

The client data field is used by the HTML parser and generator.

entries.name: clientData=
entries.code:
proc clientData=(n: XmlNode; data: int) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Sets the client data of n.

The client data field is used by the HTML parser and generator.

entries.name: addEscaped
entries.code:
proc addEscaped(result: var string; s: string) {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: The same as result.add(escape(s)), but more efficient.

entries.name: escape
entries.code: proc escape(s: string): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Escapes s for inclusion into an XML document.

Escapes these characters:

charis converted to
<<
>>
&&
""
''

You can also use addEscaped proc.

entries.name: add
entries.code:
proc add(result: var string; n: XmlNode; indent = 0; indWidth = 2;
         addNewLines = true) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Adds the textual representation of n to string result.

entries.code: proc `$`(n: XmlNode): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Converts n into its string representation.

No <$xml ...$> declaration is produced, so that the produced XML fragments are composable.

entries.name: child
entries.code:
proc child(n: XmlNode; name: string): XmlNode {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Finds the first child element of n with a name of name. Returns nil on failure.

entries.name: findAll
entries.code:
proc findAll(n: XmlNode; tag: string; result: var seq[XmlNode];
             caseInsensitive = false) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Iterates over all the children of n returning those matching tag.

Found nodes satisfying the condition will be appended to the result sequence.

entries.name: findAll
entries.code:
proc findAll(n: XmlNode; tag: string; caseInsensitive = false): seq[XmlNode] {.
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: A shortcut version to assign in let blocks.

entries.name: `<>`
entries.code: macro `<>`(x: untyped): untyped
entries.description:
Constructor macro for XML. Example usage:

<>a(href="https://nim-lang.org", newText("Nim rules."))

Produces an XML tree for:

<a href="https://nim-lang.org">Nim rules.</a>
